vulnerability.title: Implement STIG Sub Crypto Policy
vulnerability.description: Create a custom cryptographic policy to follow the guidance from DISA.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: cat << 'EOF' > /etc/crypto-policies/policies/modules/STIG.pmod cipher@SSH=AES-256-GCM AES-256-CTR AES-128-GCM AES-128-CTR mac@SSH=HMAC-SHA2-512 HMAC-SHA2-256 EOF sudo update-crypto-policies --set FIPS:STIG

triage_decision.reason: The remediation follows a standard DISA STIG cryptographic policy change that is widely used and documented. It does not involve partitioning, bootloader, or kernel changes, and is unlikely to lock out SSH or console access when applied correctly.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; networking affected; Potential temporary SSH connectivity issues if clients do not support the new ciphers or MACs defined in the policy

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for fips_custom_stig_sub_policy (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_fips_custom_stig_sub_policy). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message:
I attempted to read and modify the cryptographic policy files and restart services on the target system, but all commands failed with “Connection closed” errors. Since I cannot actually execute the required `run_cmd`, `read_file`, or `write_file` operations, I cannot apply the remediation at this time.

If you can provide a working environment or further permissions, I can re‑attempt the following steps:

1. Ensure `/etc/crypto-policies/policies/modules/STIG.pmod` contains the single line  
   ```
   cipher@SSH=AES-256-GCM AES-256-CTR AES-128-GCM AES-128-CTR mac@SSH=HMAC-SHA2-512 HMAC-SHA2-256
   ```
2. Run `update-crypto-policies --set FIPS:STIG` and verify a zero exit status.
3. Verify the active policy with `update-crypto-policies --show`.
4. Restart `sshd` with `systemctl restart sshd`.

Please let me know once I can execute these commands safely.

reasoning_messages.content:
I attempted to read and modify the cryptographic policy files and restart services on the target system, but all commands failed with “Connection closed” errors. Since I cannot actually execute the required `run_cmd`, `read_file`, or `write_file` operations, I cannot apply the remediation at this time.

If you can provide a working environment or further permissions, I can re‑attempt the following steps:

1. Ensure `/etc/crypto-policies/policies/modules/STIG.pmod` contains the single line  
   ```
   cipher@SSH=AES-256-GCM AES-256-CTR AES-128-GCM AES-128-CTR mac@SSH=HMAC-SHA2-512 HMAC-SHA2-256
   ```
2. Run `update-crypto-policies --set FIPS:STIG` and verify a zero exit status.
3. Verify the active policy with `update-crypto-policies --show`.
4. Restart `sshd` with `systemctl restart sshd`.

Please let me know once I can execute these commands safely.

review_feedback: The current proposed remediation plan was not provided in the submission, so it cannot be determined whether it addresses the previously noted issues. Without the actual plan content, a definitive assessment of effectiveness, format correctness, verification steps, or service restarts cannot be made.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan will resolve the vulnerability by adding the required STIG policy module and activating it. It is functional but could be slightly refined for robustness and clarity.
concerns: The proposed file content uses one directive per line; the standard format uses a single line with space‑separated values, which might lead to unintended parsing if crypto‑policies expects a single string per section.
The plan does not perform a post‑write verification of the file content or confirm that the update‑crypto‑policies command succeeded; a check of the exit status would be prudent.
No service restart (e.g., sshd) is performed; although update‑crypto‑policies may handle it implicitly, a restart can guarantee the new policies take effect immediately.
suggested_improvements: Write the STIG.pmod file with the exact format shown in the recommendation (all directives on a single line separated by spaces).
Add a step to validate the file contents match the expected string before and after writing.
Check the exit status of update-crypto-policies and, if required, explicitly restart services such as sshd to ensure the new policies are active.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The current proposed remediation plan was not provided in the submission, so it cannot be determined whether it addresses the previously noted issues. Without the actual plan content, a definitive assessment of effectiveness, format correctness, verification steps, or service restarts cannot be made.
concerns: Missing actual remediation steps; cannot confirm correct file format or command usage
No evidence of file content verification or exit status checks
No explicit service restart or confirmation that policies are applied
suggested_improvements: Include the full remediation script or command sequence in the plan. Ensure that the STIG.pmod file is written using a single space‑separated line. Add a step that checks the exit status of `update-crypto-policies` and validates the file contents before and after modification. Explicitly restart or reload affected services (e.g., sshd) to apply the new policy. Verify that FIPS mode activates successfully with `update-crypto-policies --show`.
